title: cyrtanthus sanguineus subsp. sanguineus
intro: Cyrtanthus sanguineus subsp. sanguineus, also known as blood lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, characterized by its bright red or reddish-brown flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cyrtanthus sanguineus subsp. sanguineus are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory tract infections, and promote the removal of mucus. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
